bullets.text: Progressive posted its 22nd annual underwriting profit, achieving a 10.7% underwriting profit margin in 1993 versus 3.5% in 1992; ROE reached 36.0%, up from 34.7%, a company record.
Net premiums written grew 25% to $1,819.2 million from $1,451.2 million in 1992, with Core business NPW up 25% to $1,700 million.
Core underwriting expense ratio fell to 25.9% from 28.4% in 1992, an improvement of 2.5 points, part of a multi-year drive toward a 20% expense ratio target by 2000.
Loss costs were 63.7% of earned premium; loss adjustment expense fell to 9.1% from 10.7% in 1992, driven by Immediate Response claims service improvements and faster claim closures.

Progressive raised $177 million in common stock and $350 million in long-term debt to support growth; invested assets grew to $2,786.4 million, and total investment income was $177.2 million after tax.
Progressive tested multi-channel Community Marketing in Miami and Tampa—agents, company offices, telephone, and mail—with encouraging results; independent agents remained the dominant channel.

Progressive reduced average private passenger auto rates by 0.8% in 1993 by lowering expenses and controlling losses, reversing a trend of annual increases and expanding standard/preferred product tests to $64 million.
Immediate Response claims service expanded with 24/7 in-person response; claims closed within 7 days of accident improved from 51% in 1992 to 56% in 1993.

Management articulated a strategic pivot from nonstandard niche insurer to broad-based auto insurer for all drivers, targeting a sustainable 4% underwriting margin while reducing consumer costs through process re-engineering.
Progressive began formal brand development in 1993, testing TV, radio, print, and direct mail to build a distinctive Progressive brand identity focused on buying, ownership, and claims experiences.
